testedConfigurations: Android 13 running Honeywell Mobility Edge CN80G with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 660 (SDM660) 32-bit without PAA Android 13 running Honeywell Mobility Edge CN80G with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 660 (SDM660) 64-bit without PAA Android 13 running on Honeywell Mobility Edge CN80G with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 660 (SDM660) 32-bit with PAA Android 13 running on Honeywell Mobility Edge CN80G with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 660 (SDM660) 64-bit with PAA Android 13 running on Honeywell Mobility Edge CT47 with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 6490 (QCM6490) 32-bit with PAA Android 13 running on Honeywell Mobility Edge CT47 with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 6490 (QCM6490) 32-bit without PAA Android 13 running on Honeywell Mobility Edge CT47 with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 6490 (QCM6490) 64-bit with PAA Android 13 running on Honeywell Mobility Edge CT47 with Qualcomm® Snapdragon 6490 (QCM6490) 64-bit without PAA
certificate.caveat: No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs (e.g., keys). When operated in approved mode.
certificate.description: Honeywell Mobility Edge™ Boring SSL cryptographic module is a library that provides FIPS 140-3 approved cryptographic algorithms for the Boring SSL and other user-space Android applications which require cryptographic functionality. The physical cryptographic boundary is Honeywell Android mobile computer on which this module is installed.

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: AES, KTS FIPS 197 SP800-38F
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Transport per IG D.G Key establishment methodology provides between 128 and 256 bits of encryption strength

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: TLS v1.0/1.1 and v1.2 KDF2 SP800-135rev1
CKG
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: Cryptographic Key Generation: Section 5: Generation of Key Pairs for Asymmetric-Key Algorithms, Section 6.1: The “Direct Generation” of Symmetric Keys
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Generation Symmetric keys and seeds are generated as the direct output of the DRBG

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: DRBG SP800-90Arev1
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: AES-256; Key size: 256 bits; Strength: 256 bits

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: ECDSA FIPS 186-4
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.modeMethod: Key Pair Generation, Signature Generation, Signature Verification, Public Key Validation

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: HMAC FIPS 198-1
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: HMAC-SHA-1, HMAC-SHA2-224, HMAC-SHA2-256, HMAC-SHA2-384, HMAC-SHA2-512; Strength: 128, 192, 256, 384, 512 bits

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: RSA FIPS 186-4
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.modeMethod: Key Generation, Signature Generation, Signature Verification PKCS 1.5 and PSS
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: 1024, 2048, 3072, 4096; Strength: 80, 112, 128, 152 bits; Note: Key size 1024 should be only used for Signature Verification

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: SHA FIPS 180-4
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Digital Signature Generation, Digital Signature Verification, Non-Digital Signature Applications

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: KAS-SSC SP800-56Arev3
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Key Agreement Scheme Shared Secret Computation per SP800-56Arev3; Key establishment methodology provides between 112 and 256 bits of security strength

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: MD5
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: As allowed per SP800-135rev1 (No security claimed)

extraColumns.Use / Function: When used with the TLS protocol version 1.0 and 1.1

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: Use of a [SP800-90B] compliant entropy source with at least 256 bits of security strength. Entropy is supplied to the Module via callback functions. The callback functions shall return an error if the minimum entropy strength cannot be met. The caveat “No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs (e.g., keys)” is applicable

Use of a [SP800-90B] compliant entropy source with at least 256 bits of security strength. Entropy is supplied to the Module via callback functions. The callback functions shall return an error if the minimum entropy strength cannot be met. The caveat “No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs (e.g., keys)” is applicable
